title: Entity 72
wikitext:
__NOTOC__{{Poolrooms Theme}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity 72: "Foams"}}{{Entity Class|Int=3|Dan=5|no=72|Agg=5|Hab=Humid and aquatic levels}} == Description == {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Foam 1.png|caption=A photograph of a Foam in whats looks like [[Level 28]] or [[The Attic]].}}'''Entity 72''', widely known as "'''Foams'''," are entities found in a majority of levels in [[the Backrooms]], but most specifically in humid, aquatic, or enclosed levels. '''Foams''' are sentient, average-sized blobs of soap foam with excellent senses. In particular, '''Foams''' possess six retractable tentacles through which they move, grab, and feel touch. Most notably, a '''Foam''''s body is coated with a makeup of highly acidic substances—like sulfuric and hydrochloric acids—that it uses to digest prey instantly. A '''Foam''''s body will generally act like a non-Newtonian fluid, making it effectively immune to physical attacks. Although it could hypothetically be killed by getting slashed in half, the '''Foam''' will also clone itself when cut, with its severed parts becoming new instances. These two instances may also merge to create one bigger, faster, and more deadly '''Foam'''. Instead, to destroy a '''Foam''', its acid layers must be neutralized with a strong base, and then its body can be dissolved using water or white vinegar. '''Foams''' can also be killed when exposed to high temperatures because their bodies evaporate. == Behaviors == {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Foam 2.png|caption=A Foam preparing to attack.}}'''Foams''' are highly aggressive and will hunt for just about any non-'''Foam''' in their general proximity. '''Foams''' will act in a friendly and cooperative manner with each other, working together to build dens and hunt prey. Despite being primarily nomadic, '''Foams''' occasionally build dens to store food and tools. '''Foams''' will occasionally slice themselves to create more instances or merge with others specimens to become one stronger '''Foam'''. Strangely enough, '''Foams''' are attracted to caffeinated drinks. When consumed, the '''Foam''''s body's acidity is increased, and it becomes more aggravated and agitated. '''Foams''' usually target unknowing humans and small entities as their primary prey. To catch prey, '''Foams''' will either stick to the ceiling or stay completely immobile to look harmless. '''Foams''' may also produce a wide range of sounds to attract their prey closer. Once the target is close enough, they will jump onto it and hold tight using their tentacles until the prey is properly digested. To survive a '''Foam''', the main strategy would be to carry strong bases and any foam-diluting liquid and train running. == Biology == {{Blurimage|file=Foam 3.png|caption=A Foam cut in half turning into two.|align=right}}Essentially, a '''Foam''' is a hyper-flexible, sentient, non-Newtonian blob of soap foam coated in a wide variety of both acidic and non-acidic substances. The '''Foam''''s tentacles are its main tool to survive, the primary tools it uses to hunt, grab, and feel around it. In its tentacles lie multiple structures that grant '''Foams''' the ability to sense smell, touch, and more through their tentacles. The tentacles also contain sticky, glue-like substances to help them stick to ceilings or prey, making their hunting quicker and more effective. '''Foams''' are notable for their exceptional senses. They can hear from miles away and sense subtle movements, pressure, and temperature changes; they have highly sensitive pain sensors and excellent balance. Although blind, '''Foams''' use echolocation and subtle chemical changes in the air to detect nearby prey. '''Foams''' can change their acidity at will by brewing and mixing the substances on their surface—that catch on them with time, like water—with the acid coats around them, changing their acidity and corrosiveness to avoid dissolving the surfaces they walk on or even their bodies. Due to this, the older a '''Foam''''s body material is and the more substances that catch on it, the more control it has over its acids. This means that, hypothetically, cleaning materials or clean water could be used to neutralize this ability in the long term. If a '''Foam''' comes into contact with too much water, its body will dilute in it, dispersing its substances throughout. In this state, the '''Foam''' becomes much weaker and more vulnerable. It becomes slower, easier to kill, and less acidic as it balances with the water around it. However, with this predicament, '''Foam''' gains the ability to manipulate all the water that extends it. For example, if a '''Foam''' falls into a lake, it functionally gains control over the entire lake's extent and any river that connects to it. Diluted '''Foams''' are relatively easy to kill; if they receive enough water—which varies based on their size—they will be spread out so much their parts can no longer communicate. A Foam's diet consists of just about anything with nutrients; its acid layers decompose nearly anything they touch with the right acidity and corrosiveness. However, '''Foams''' usually tend to value food like flesh, water, and plants. '''Foams''' require a high amount of nutrients to function due to the nature of their anatomy and practices, which require a high metabolic rate and the constant need for more energy. Therefore, they have to eat every few minutes to maintain their bodies and functions. == Discovery == Although the exact date of '''Foam''''s discovery is unknown, it is believed to be around the mid-1900s, with multiple cases describing entities similar to '''Foams''', especially on levels such as [[Level 28]] and [[Level 30]]. During that time, these claims were dismissed due to a lack of evidence and very few survivors. == Containment and Weakening Methods == {{Blurimage|file=Foam 4.png|align=right|caption=A Foam in a container.}}Since its addition to the entity catalog, many groups such as [[M.E.G.]], [[B.W.O.]], and others have attempted to develop their own '''Foam''' containment methods for scientific research and military purposes. They are usually small acid-resistant plastic containers filled with water inside to slowly dilute the '''Foam''', weakening it. They usually retain sticky and slimy substances on their surfaces that prevent the '''Foams''' from escaping. Although these methods are highly effective, they are expensive and not usually renewable. Renewable containment and weakening methods include water buckets, sprays, and simple traps made from acid-resistant materials. These are usually less effective, but they are the cheapest and most practical methods for the ordinary wanderer to use and make. == Do's and Don'ts: == === Do: === * Run from it. * Break its acid layers and dissolve its inner body. * Dilute it with water. * Stay away from any area it is known to inhabit. * Attempt to contain it. === Don't: === * Come into physical contact with it. * Slice it in half. * Let two of them merge. * Let it consume caffeine.
